preamble: The shift from the postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the voiceless interdental fricative [θ] is a form of fronting and depalatalization. It involves moving the tongue tip forward from the postalveolar region to the teeth. This is common in developmental speech and was a factor in several historical sibilant mergers.

examples.note: Developmental fronting: replacing a hushing sibilant with an interdental fricative.
Part of the complex 16th-century readjustment where earlier coronal fricatives moved forward to the interdental position.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
